author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm IX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The inscription of this Psalm is, “To the end for the hidden things of the Son, a Psalm of David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As to the hidden things of the Son there may be a question: but since he has not added whose, the very only-begotten Son of God should be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For where a Psalm has been inscribed of the son of David, “When,” he says, “he fled from the face of Absalom his son;” although his name even was mentioned, and therefore there could be no obscurity as to whom it was spoken of: yet it is not merely said, from the face of son Absalom; but “his” is added.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But here both because “his” is not added, and much is said of the Gentiles, it cannot properly be taken of Absalom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the war which that abandoned one waged with his father, no way relates to the Gentiles, since there the people of Israel only were divided against themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm is then sung for the hidden things of the only-begotten Son of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord Himself too, when, without addition, He uses the word Son, would have Himself, the Only-begotten to be understood; as where He says, “If the Son shall make you free, then shall ye be free indeed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He said not, the Son of God; but in saying merely, Son, He gives us to understand whose Son it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which form of expression nothing admits of, save His excellency of whom we so speak, that, though we name Him not, He can be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so we say, it rains, clears up, thunders, and such like expressions; and we do not add who does it all; for that the excellency of the doer spontaneously presents itself to all men’s minds, and does not want words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then are the hidden things of the Son?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By which expression we must first understand that there are some things of the Son manifest, from which those are distinguished which are called hidden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore since we believe two advents of the Lord, one past, which the Jews understood not: the other future, which we both hope for; and since the one which the Jews understood not, profited the Gentiles; “For the hidden things of the Son” is not unsuitably understood to be spoken of this advent, in which “blindness in part is happened to Israel, that the fulness of the Gentiles might come in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For notice of two judgments is conveyed to us throughout the Scriptures, if any one will give heed to them, one hidden, the other manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hidden one is passing now, of which the Apostle Peter says, “The time is come that judgment should begin from the house of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hidden judgment accordingly is the pain, by which now each man is either exercised to purification, or warned to conversion, or if he despise the calling and discipline of God, is blinded unto damnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the manifest judgment is that in which the Lord, at His coming, will judge the quick and the dead, all men confessing that it is He by whom both rewards shall be assigned to the good, and punishments to the evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But then that confession will avail, not to the remedy of evils, but to the accumulation of damnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of these two judgments, the one hidden, the other manifest, the Lord seems to me to have spoken, where He says, “Whoso believeth on Me hath passed from death unto life, and shall not come into judgment;” into the manifest judgment, that is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which passes from death unto life by means of some affliction, whereby “He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth,” is the hidden judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But whoso believeth not,” saith He, “hath been judged already:” that is, by this hidden judgment hath been already prepared for that manifest one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These two judgments we read of also in Wisdom, whence it is written, “Therefore unto them, as to children without the use of reason, Thou didst give a judgment to mock them; But they that have not been corrected by this judgment have felt a judgment worthy of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso then are not corrected by this hidden judgment of God, shall most worthily be punished by that manifest one.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will confess unto Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not, with a whole heart, confess unto God, who doubteth of His Providence in any particular: but he who sees already the hidden things of the wisdom of God, how great is His invisible reward, who saith, “We rejoice in tribulations;” and how all torments, which are inflicted on the body, are either for the exercising of those that are converted to God, or for warning that they be converted, or for just preparation of the obdurate unto their last damnation: and so now all things are referred to the governance of Divine Providence, which fools think done as it were by chance and at random, and without any Divine ordering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will tell all Thy marvels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He tells all God’s marvels, who sees them performed not only openly on the body, but invisibly indeed too in the soul, but far more sublimely and excellently.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men earthly, and led wholly by the eye, marvel more that the dead Lazarus rose again in the body, than that Paul the persecutor rose again in soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since the visible miracle calleth the soul to the light, but the invisible enlighteneth the soul that comes when called, he tells all God’s marvels, who, by believing the visible, passes on to the understanding of the invisible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will be glad and exult in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not any more in this world, not in pleasure of bodily dalliance, not in relish of palate and tongue, not in sweetness of perfumes, not in joyousness of passing sounds, not in the variously coloured forms of figure, not in vanities of men’s praise, not in wedlock and perishable offspring, not in superfluity of temporal wealth, not in this world’s getting, whether it extend over place and space, or be prolonged in time’s succession: but, “I will be glad and exult in Thee,” namely, in the hidden things of the Son, where “the light of Thy countenance hath been stamped on us, O Lord:” for, “Thou wilt hide them,” saith he, “in the hiding place of Thy countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then will be glad and exult in Thee, who tells all Thy marvels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He will tell all Thy marvels (since it is now spoken of prophetically), “who came not to do His own will, but the will of Him who sent Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now the Person of the Lord begins to appear speaking in this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it follows, “I will sing to Thy Name, O Most High, in turning mine enemy behind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His enemy then, where was he turned back?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it when it was said to him, “Get thee behind, Satan”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For then he who by tempting desired to put himself before, was turned behind, by failing in deceiving Him who was tempted, and by availing nothing against Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For earthly men are behind: but the heavenly man is preferred before, although he came after.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the first man is of the earth, earthy: the second Man is from heaven, heavenly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But from this stock he came by whom it was said, “He who cometh after me is preferred before me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Apostle forgets “those things that are behind, and reaches forth unto those things that are before.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The enemy, therefore, was turned behind, after that he could not deceive the heavenly Man being tempted; and he turned himself to earthy men, where he can have dominion.…For in truth the devil is turned behind, even in the persecution of the righteous, and he, much more to their advantage, is a persecutor, than if he went before as a leader and a prince.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We must sing then to the Name of the Most High in turning the enemy behind: since we ought to choose rather to fly from him as a persecutor, than to follow him as a leader.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we have whither we may fly and hide ourselves in the hidden things of the Son; seeing that “the Lord hath been made a refuge for us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They will be weakened, and perish from Thy face” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who will be weakened and perish, but the unrighteous and ungodly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They will be weakened,” while they shall avail nothing; “and they shall perish,” because the ungodly will not be; “from the face” of God, that is, from the knowledge of God, as he perished who said, “But now I live not, but Christ liveth in me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why will the ungodly “be weakened and perish from thy face?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because,” he saith, “Thou hast made my judgment, and my cause:” that is, the judgment in which I seemed to be judged, Thou hast made mine; and the cause in which men condemned me just and innocent, Thou hast made mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For such things served Him for our deliverance: as sailors too call the wind theirs, which they take advantage of for prosperous sailing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou satest on the throne Who judgest equity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether the Son say this to the Father, who said also, “Thou couldest have no power against Me, except it were given thee from above,” referring this very thing, that the Judge of men was judged for men’s advantage, to the Father’s equity and His own hidden things: or whether man say to God, “Thou satest on the throne Who judgest equity,” giving the name of God’s throne to his soul, so that his body may peradventure be the earth, which is called God’s “footstool:” for “God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto Himself:” or whether the soul of the Church, perfect now and without spot and wrinkle, worthy, that is, of the hidden things of the Son, in that “the King hath brought her into His chamber,” say to her spouse, “Thou satest upon the throne Who judgest equity,” in that Thou hast risen from the dead, and ascended up into heaven, and sittest at the right hand of the Father: whichsoever, I say, of those opinions, whereunto this verse may be referred, is preferred, it transgresses not the rule of faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast rebuked the heathen, and the ungodly hath perished” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We take this to be more suitably said to the Lord Jesus Christ, than said by Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who else hath rebuked the heathen, and the ungodly perished, save He, who after that He ascended up into heaven, sent the Holy Ghost, that, filled by Him, the Apostles should preach the word of God with boldness, and freely reprove men’s sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At which rebuke the ungodly perished; because the ungodly was justified and was made godly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast effaced their name for the world, and for the world’s world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The name of the ungodly hath been effaced.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are not called ungodly who believe in the true God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now their name is effaced “for the world,” that is, as long as the course of the temporal world endures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And for the world’s world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “the world’s world,” but that whose image and shadow, as it were, this world possesses?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the change of seasons succeeding one another, whilst the moon is on the wane, and again on the increase, whilst the sun each year returns to his quarter, whilst spring, or summer, or autumn, or winter passes away only to return, is in some sort an imitation of eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this world’s world is that which abides in immutable eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As a verse in the mind, and a verse in the voice, the former is understood, the latter heard; and the former fashions the latter; and hence the former works in art and abides, the latter sounds in the air and passes away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So the fashion of this changeable world is defined by that world unchangeable which is called the world’s world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hence the one abides in the art, that is, in the Wisdom and Power of God: but the other is made to pass in the governance of creation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If after all it be not a repetition, so that after it was said “for the world,” lest it should be understood of this world that passeth away, it were added “for the world’s world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the Greek copies it is thus, εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα, καὶ εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τοῦ αἰῶνος Which the Latins have for the most rendered, not, “for the world, and for the world’s world;” but, “for ever, and for the world’s world,” that in the words “for the world’s world,” the, words “for ever,” should be explained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The name,” then, “of the ungodly Thou hast effaced for ever,” for from henceforth the ungodly shall never be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if their name be not prolonged unto this world, much less unto the world’s world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The swords of the enemy have failed at the end” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not enemies in the plural, but this enemy in the singular.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now what enemy’s swords have failed but the devil’s?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now these are understood to be divers erroneous opinions, whereby as with swords he destroys souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In overcoming these swords, and in bringing them to failure, that sword is employed, of which it is said in the seventh Psalm, “If ye be not converted, He will brandish His sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And peradventure this is the end, against which the swords of the enemy fail; since up to it they are of some avail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now it worketh secretly, but in the last judgment it will be brandished openly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By it the cities are destroyed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so it follows, “The swords of the enemy have failed at the end: and Thou hast destroyed the cities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cities indeed wherein the devil rules, where crafty and deceitful counsels hold, as it were, the place of a court, on which supremacy attend as officers and ministers the services of all the members, the eyes for curiosity, the ears for lasciviousness, or for whatsoever else is gladly listened to that bears on evil, the hands for rapine or any other violence or pollution soever, and all the other members after this manner serving the tyrannical supremacy, that is, perverse counsels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this city the commonalty, as it were, are all soft affections and disturbing emotions of the mind, stirring up daily seditions in a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then where a king, where a court, where ministers, where commonalty are found, there is a city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now again would such things be in bad cities, unless they were first in individual men, who are, as it were, the elements and seeds of cities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These cities He destroys, when on the prince being shut out thence, of whom it was said, “The prince of this world” has been “cast out,” these kingdoms are wasted by the word of truth, evil counsels are laid to sleep, vile affections tamed, the ministries of the members and senses taken captive, and transferred to the service of righteousness and good works: that as the Apostle says, “Sin should no more reign in” our “mortal body,” and so forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then is the soul at peace, and the man is disposed to receive rest and blessedness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their memorial has perished with uproar:” with the uproar, that is, of the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is said, “with uproar,” either because when ungodliness is overturned, there is uproar made: for none passeth to the highest place, where there is the deepest silence, but he who with much uproar shall first have warred with his own vices: or “with uproar,” is said, that the memory of the ungodly should perish in the perishing even of the very uproar, in which ungodliness riots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord abideth for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore” then “have the heathen raged, and the people imagined vain things against the Lord, and against His anointed:” for “the Lord abideth for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath prepared His seat in judgment, and He shall judge the world in equity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He prepared His seat when He was judged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by that patience Man purchased heaven, and God in Man profited believers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is the Son’s hidden judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But seeing He is also to come openly and in the sight of all to judge the quick and the dead, He hath prepared His seat in the hidden judgment: and He shall also openly “judge the world in equity:” that is, He shall distribute gifts proportioned to desert, setting the sheep on His right hand, and the goats on His left.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall judge the people with justice”(ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the same as was said above, “He shall judge the world in equity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as men judge who see not the heart, by whom very often worse men are acquitted than are condemned: but “in equity” and “with justice” shall the Lord judge, “conscience bearing witness, and thoughts accusing, or else excusing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord hath become a refuge to the poor” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever be the persecutions of that enemy, who hath been turned behind, what harm shall he do to them whose refuge the Lord hath become?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this will be, if in this world, in which that one has an office of power, they shall choose to be poor, by loving nothing which either here leaves a man while he lives and loves, or is left by him when he dies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to such a poor man hath the Lord become a refuge, “an Helper in due season, in tribulation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, He maketh poor, for “He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what “an Helper in due season” is, he explained by adding “in tribulation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the soul is not turned to God, save when it is turned away from this world: nor is it more seasonably turned away from this world, except toils and pains be mingled with its trifling and hurtful and destructive pleasures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let them who know Thy Name, hope in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10), when they shall have ceased hoping in wealth, and in the other enticements of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the soul indeed that seeketh where to fix her hope, when she is torn away from this world, the knowledge of God’s Name seasonably receives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the mere Name of God hath now been published everywhere: but the knowledge of the name is, when He is known whose name it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the name is not a name for its own sake, but for that which it signifies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now it has been said, “The Lord is His Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore whoso willingly submits himself to God as His servant, hath known this name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let them who know Thy Name hope in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, the Lord saith to Moses, “I am That I am; and Thou shalt say to the children of Israel, I Am, hath sent me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them” then “who know Thy Name, hope in Thee;” that they may not hope in those things which flow by in time’s quick revolution, having nothing but “will be” and “has been.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what in them is future, when it arrives, straightway becomes the past; it is awaited with eagerness, it is lost with pain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the nature of God nothing will be, as if it were not yet; or hath been, as if it were no longer: but there is only that which is, and this is eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them cease then to hope in and love things temporal, and let them apply themselves to hope eternal, who know His name who said, “I am That I am;” and of whom it was said, “I Am hath sent me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast not forsaken them that seek Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso seek Him, seek no more things transient and perishable; “For no man can serve two masters.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing to the Lord, who dwelleth in Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11), is said to them, whom the Lord forsakes not as they seek Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He dwelleth in Sion, which is interpreted watching, and which beareth the likeness of the Church that now is; as Jerusalem beareth the likeness of the Church that is to come, that is, the city of Saints already enjoying life angelical; for Jerusalem is by interpretation the vision of peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now watching goes before vision, as this Church goes before that one which is promised, the city immortal and eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in time it goes before, not in dignity: because more honourable is that whither we are striving to arrive, than what we practise, that we may attain to arrive; now we practise watching, that we may arrive at vision.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But again this same Church which now is, unless the Lord inhabit her, the most earnest watching might run into any sort of error.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And to this Church it was said, “For the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are:” again, “that Christ may dwell in the inner man in your hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is enjoined us then, that we sing to the Lord who dwelleth in Sion, that with one accord we praise the Lord, the Inhabitant of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Show forth His wonders among the heathen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It has both been done, and will not cease to be done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For requiring their blood He hath remembered” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if they, who were sent to preach the Gospel, should make answer to that injunction which has been mentioned, “Show forth His wonders among the heathen,” and should say, “O Lord, who hath believed our report?” and again, “For Thy sake we are killed all the day long;” the Psalmist suitably goes on to say, That Christians not without great reward of eternity will die in persecution, “for requiring their blood He hath remembered.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why did he choose to say, “their blood”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it, as if one of imperfect knowledge and less faith should ask, How will they “show them forth,” seeing that the infidelity of the heathen will rage against them; and he should be answered, “For requiring their blood He hath remembered,” that is, the last judgment will come, in which both the glory of the slain and the punishment of the slayers shall be made manifest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let no one suppose “He hath remembered” to be so used, as though forgetfulness can attach to God; but since the judgment will be after a long interval, it is used in accordance with the feeling of weak men, who think God hath forgotten, because He doth not act so speedily as they wish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To such is said what follows also, “He hath not forgotten the cry of the poor:” that is, He hath not, as you suppose, forgotten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if they should on hearing, “He hath remembered,” say, Then He had forgotten; No, “He hath not forgotten,” says the Psalmist, “the cry of the poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I ask, what is that cry of the poor, which God forgetteth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it that cry, the words whereof are these, “Pity me, O Lord, see my humiliation at the hands of my enemies”? (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then did he not say, Pity “us” O Lord, see our humiliation at the hands of “our” enemies, as if many poor were crying; but as if one, Pity “me,” O Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it because One intercedeth for the Saints, “who” first “for our sakes became poor, though He was rich;” and it is He who saith, “Who exaltest me from the gates of death (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 14), that I may declare all Thy praises in the gates of the daughter of Sion”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For man is exalted in Him, not that Man only which He beareth, which is the Head of the Church; but whichsoever one of us also is among the other members, and is exalted from all depraved desires; which are the gates of death, for that through them is the road to death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the joy in the fruition is at once death itself, when one gains what he hath in abandoned wilfulness coveted: for “coveting is the root of all evil:” and therefore is the gate of death, for “the widow that liveth in pleasures is dead.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At which pleasures we arrive through desires as it were through the gates of death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But all highest purposes are the gates of the daughter of Sion, through which we come to the vision of peace in the Holy Church.…Or haply are the gates of death the bodily senses and eyes, which were opened when the man tasted of the forbidden tree,… and are the gates of the daughter of Sion the sacraments and beginnings of faith, which are opened to them that knock, that they may arrive at the hidden things of the Son?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then follows, “I will exult for Thy salvation:” that is, with blessedness shall I be holden by Thy salvation, which is our Lord Jesus Christ, the Power and Wisdom of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore says the Church, which is here in affliction and is saved by hope, as long as the hidden judgment of the Son is, in hope she says, “I will exult for Thy salvation:” for now she is worn down either by the roar of violence around her, or by the errors of the heathen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heathen are fixed in the corruption, which they made” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider ye how punishment is reserved for the sinner, out of his own works; and how they that have wished to persecute the Church, have been fixed in that corruption, which they thought to inflict.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were desiring to kill the body, whilst they themselves were dying in soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In that snare which they hid, has their foot been taken.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hidden snare is crafty devising.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The foot of the soul is well understood to be its love: which, when depraved, is called coveting or lust; but when upright, love or charity.…And the Apostle says, “That being rooted and grounded in love, ye may be able to take in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The foot then of sinners, that is, their love, is taken in the snare, which they hide: for when delight shall have followed on to deceitful dealing, when God shall have delivered them over to the lust of their heart; that delight at once binds them, that they dare not tear away their love thence and apply it to profitable objects; for when they shall make the attempt, they will be pained in heart, as if desiring to free their foot from a fetter: and giving way under this pain they refuse to withdraw from pernicious delights.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the snare” then “which they have hid,” that is, in deceitful counsel, “their foot hath been taken,” that is, their love, which through deceit attains to that vain joy whereby pain is purchased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is known executing judgments” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are God’s judgments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not from that tranquillity of His blessedness, nor from the secret places of wisdom, wherein blessed souls are received, is the sword, or fire, or wild beast, or any such thing brought forth, whereby sinners may be tormented: but how are they tormented, and how does the Lord do judgment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the works,” he says, “of his own hands hath the sinner been caught.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here is interposed, “The song of the diapsalma” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16): as it were the hidden joy, as far as we can imagine, of the separation which is now made, not in place, but in the affections of the heart, between sinners and the righteous, as of the corn from the chaff, as yet on the floor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then follows, “Let the sinners be turned into hell” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 17): that is, let them be given into their own hands, when they are spared, and let them be ensnared in deadly delight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the nations that forget God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “when they did not think good to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there shall not be forgetfulness of the poor man to the end” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 18); who now seems to be in forgetfulness, when sinners are thought to flourish in this world’s happiness, and the righteous to be in travail: but “the patience,” saith He, “of the poor shall not perish for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore there is need of patience now to bear with the evil, who are already separated in will, till they be also separated at the last judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, let not man prevail” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The future judgment is prayed for: but before it come, “Let the heathen,” saith he, “be judged in Thy sight:” that is, in secret; which is called in God’s sight, with the knowledge of a few holy and righteous ones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Place a lawgiver over them, O Lord.” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seems to me to point out Antichrist: of whom the Apostle says, “When the man of sin shall be revealed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the heathen know that they are men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they who will be set free by the Son of God, and belong to the Son of Man, and be sons of men, that is, new men, may serve man, that is, the old man the sinner, “for that they are men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because it is believed that he is to arrive at so great a pitch of empty glory, and he will be permitted to do so great things, both against all men and against the Saints of God, that then some weak ones shall indeed think that God cares not for human affairs, the Psalmist interposing a diapsalma, adds as it were the voice of men groaning and asking why judgment is deferred.
